notes: Story IDs match the spec's §Stories sketch (S1..S8). S8 in spec is a one-shot manual validation (deliberately break the reducer, observe property suite catches it, revert). It is recorded under epic_state.notes for the wrap-up step but is NOT a code story and is not in story_order.

stories.name: fast-check-dependency-wiring
stories.title: Add fast-check devDependency and `make test-property` target

in: Add fast-check ^3.23.0 (or newer compatible) to devDependencies in package.json
Run npm install to refresh package-lock.json
Add a `test-property: node_modules` target to Makefile that runs vitest on the two src/store/*.property.test.ts files with FAST_CHECK_NUM_RUNS=10000
Verify make test still passes (no new tests yet — proves wiring is correct)
Verify make test-property passes (no tests yet, exits 0 with 'no test files found' or vitest's empty-pattern handling — depending on vitest behaviour, may need a stub property test that asserts true)
out: Writing any property test bodies (those are S2..S6)
Modifying npm test to include test-property by default — make test runs default (1000) budget anyway via npm run test:unit since *.property.test.ts files match src/**/*.test.ts pattern

stories.verification_notes: AC-DEP-5 (deliberate failure flips exit code) is a one-shot sanity check, performed manually during story verification — not a committed test.

stories.name: reducer-property-tests-state-and-postconditions
stories.title: Reducer property tests: S1–S4, S8, S9, A1–A6, A13, R1

in: Build arbitraries: arbHexPubkey, arbTask, arbTaskEvent(state) (state-aware), arbEventSequence(maxLength=30)
Cover invariants S1, S2, S3, S4 (state)
Cover invariants S8 (per-group event-log isolation in createInMemoryKVStore), S9 (replay determinism)
Cover postconditions A1, A2, A3, A4, A5, A6 (stale no-op)
Cover A13 idempotence
Cover R1 resurrection caveat (labelled via fc.statistics)
Use 1000 numRuns by default; respect FAST_CHECK_NUM_RUNS env var override
Each `it` block prefixes its title with the invariant ID in brackets e.g. it('[S1] status enum closure', ...)
Each `it` body begins with a single-line comment // {ID}: {short description}
Use fc.statistics for action-type-frequency distribution (per AC-X-OBSERVABILITY-1)

stories.name: reducer-convergence-and-labelled
stories.title: Reducer property tests (continued): C1, C2, C3, D1, D3

in: Add C1 permutation-independence test (use fc.permutation; enforce strictly distinct timestamps via post-processing)
Add C2 author-irrelevance test
Add C3 duplicate-tolerance test (each event repeated 1–3 times)
Add D1 labelled property: equal-timestamp updates may diverge — fc.statistics, no assertion
Add D3 labelled property: late-arriving task.snapshot overwrites newer events — fc.statistics, no assertion
Maintain naming, comment-line, and runs-budget conventions from S2
Implement FakeBoard class: clients (TaskState[]), bus (Array<{event, deliveredTo: Set<number>}>), dispatch(clientIdx, event), deliver(busIdx, targetIdx), quiesce()
Implement arbBoardSchedule arbitrary: numClients ∈ [2, 5], interleaved dispatch+deliver ops
C0 task-state subset: post-quiesce all clients deep-equal each other
C1 permutation-independence in multi-client setting (fixed event multiset, random per-client delivery order)
C2 author-irrelevance multi-client
C3 duplicate-tolerance multi-client (deliver 1–3 times)
A13 multi-client idempotence (same event delivered to same client multiple times)
A15 multi-device same-pubkey self-converge (numClients=2, same identity)
500 numRuns default; FAST_CHECK_NUM_RUNS override; same naming / comment / observability conventions as S2
out: D1/D3 labelled properties in this harness (next story)

in: D1 multi-client: equal-timestamp updates from two clients to same task — record inter-client divergence rate via fc.statistics; no assertion
D3 multi-client: force task.snapshot delivery after later individual events on at least one client — record per-client divergence via fc.statistics; no assertion
out: Production fixes for D1/D3 (explicitly out of scope per spec §Out of Scope)

stories.name: playwright-property-spec
stories.title: Full-stack Playwright property spec via fc.commands

in: Implement ModelState (per-actor expected groups/members/tasks)
Implement RealSystem (wraps two browser contexts + pages, exposes reset(), quiesce(), dispatchCt(actor, task), getTask(actor, id), etc.)

Implement one fc.AsyncCommand class per DSL verb in scope: CgCommand, InCommand, LgCommand, FdCommand, RdCommand, CtCommand, UtCommand, ScCommand, AsCommand, UnCommand, DtCommand, RlCommand, SwCommand (Au/Dc are setup, not random)
Each Command implements check(), run(), toString() — toString returns matrix DSL form so counterexamples read as Actor.Verb(args)
Wrap fc.assert(fc.asyncProperty(fc.commands(commands, { maxCommands: 10 }), ...)) inside a single test.describe.serial block
numRuns: 20, timeout: 120_000
Per-command postconditions assert A1..A12 as relevant
After each fc.commands run: assert C0 (settled-state equality), S5 (member-iff-leaf), S7 (identity isolation post-Sw), S10 (DeviceList row count == leaf count), S6 (epoch monotonic per context)
A14 negative delivery: LgCommand and FdCommand last-leaf postconditions include a 2-second observer wait via ndk-subscriber to confirm no decryptable kind-445 events
Reuse e2e/fixtures/two-party.ts as the primary DSL home — extend it with the missing helpers below rather than building parallel scaffolding

Extend e2e/fixtures/two-party.ts with new helpers: switchIdentity(page, bunkerUrl) (the Sw verb — clicks disconnect-button, waits for sign-in, re-authenticates); disconnect(page) (the Dc verb); renameDevice(page, deviceLabel, newName); quiesceFor(group, opts) (relay-drain quiescence — see exploration-s6-deep-dive.json for the existing inline patterns); a scoped leaveGroupByName(page, name)
Use dispatchTaskEvent (not the UI helpers) for all task mutations in Commands — addTaskViaUi/moveTaskToNext/deleteTaskViaUi ignore their identifier args (per deep-dive)
out: Modifying any helper outside e2e/fixtures/two-party.ts and e2e/fixtures/ndk-subscriber.ts (auth helpers, cleanup, bunker.mjs are off-limits)
Removing or skipping any existing example-based spec
Backporting the new helpers' usage into existing example-based specs — the new helpers are added but existing tests stay as-is

in: Append `## Property-test coverage` section to docs/two-party-permutation-matrix.md with the TP-XX → invariant table from spec.md §Documentation

Create e2e/tests/property-tests.md covering: three-layer model (reducer → multi-client → full stack), invariant catalogue cross-reference, how to read fast-check counterexamples, how to reproduce via FAST_CHECK_SEED, distinction between asserted (S/A/C) and labelled (D) properties, why D1/D2/D3 are labelled-only
